QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_56

question.text: In the context of Electronic Contract Notes (ECNs), if delivery fails (email bounces), the broker must:

answer.text: Send a physical copy within the stipulated time under extant SEBI/exchange regulations
Try sending the email again the next day
Inform the exchange and take no further action
Upload the contract note to the broker's website only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When ECNs have not been delivered or have bounced, the member must send a physical copy to the client within the stipulated time. The broker must maintain a bounce mail log showing details of undelivered contract notes. **The Trap:** ECNs must be digitally signed, encrypted, non-tamperable, and comply with the IT Act 2000. They can also be sent via SMS/electronic instant messaging on the registered mobile number. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII covers contract note delivery obligations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ECN requirements: client opt-in, digital signature, encryption, non-tamper proof, bounce log maintenance, simultaneous web publication. If email bounces → physical copy mandatory. This ensures no client misses their trade confirmations regardless of delivery method.
